title: grastidium incumbens
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Grastidium incumbens benefits include reduced inflammation and bleeding of mucous membranes and gums, as well as inhibited oxidative burst and release of proteases and chemokines.
side_effects: Grastidium incumbens can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with medications. If ingested in excess, it can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure can lead to headaches and dizziness. In rare cases, improper use of Grastidium incumbens may also result in skin irritation, itching, and anaphylaxis in individuals with severe allergies. It's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes to avoid these potential side effects.
